headline: France to reimburse cadmium tests, but doctors warn access is 'too restrictive'
subhead: This carcinogenic heavy metal affects most people living in France, but the screening, expected to be reimbursed by the government starting this summer, will be limited to those considered 'potentially overexposed' because of where they live.

subhead: The suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, who is set to appear in court as early as Monday, wrote a manifesto in which he expressed his anger toward the government and mocked the event's security measures.

headline: Wembanyama, in his return after a concussion, leads Spurs to erase a 19-point deficit, beating Portland
subhead: The 22-year-old French player had missed game three of the series after a head injury he sustained in game two. In their victory over Portland, he scored 27 points for the San Antonio Spurs.

headline: Oil traders' windfall profits highlight paradoxes of Swiss model
subhead: Geneva is full of rumors about the record profits made by oil market players. The legal framework in which they operate has drawn criticism and sparked calls for tighter regulation.

headline: Vincent Lemire, historian: 'In the Israeli-Palestinian conflict, Jerusalem is not the obstacle, it could be the solution'
subhead: The historian suggests focusing on the concrete living conditions of Jerusalem's residents in order to invent new forms of coexistence between Israelis and Palestinians.

headline: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
subhead: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.
